caption: The video appears to be an educational tutorial on cryogenic engineering, specifically focusing on Tutorial 4, which discusses the location of \( h_1 \) and additional work. The slide presents a table with various thermodynamic properties such as pressure (p), temperature (T), enthalpy (h), and entropy (s) for different states labeled 1, 2, f, and g. The calculations involve determining the enthalpy at state \( h_1' \) using the efficiency (\( \varepsilon \)) formula, where \( \varepsilon = \frac{h_{1'} - h_g}{h_1 - h_g} \). In this case, \( h_{1'} \) is calculated as 438.8 J/g by substituting the given values into the equation \( h_{1'} = \varepsilon(h_1 - h_g) + h_g \). The additional work required is then computed as the difference between the initial enthalpy \( h_1 \) and the new enthalpy \( h_{1'} \), resulting in 23.2 J/g. The tutorial is presented by Prof. M D Atrey from the Department of Mechanical Engineering at IIT Bombay.
